question: Are microplastics in food and water causing serious harm to human health?
topic: Corrects overblown health claims about microplastics where evidence shows correlation but not causat

sources.name: Food Safety and Inspection Service - Microplastics in Food — FDA

discovery_sources.text: Primary route of microplastic exposure is inhalation (air) rather than ingestion - host and guest both present this as established finding
discovery_sources.origin: Unbiased Science: Dissolving the Myths on Dishwasher and Laundry Pods (See What We Did There?)

discovery_sources.text: Current detection methods for microplastics were developed specifically for water, not air, food, or body tissues
discovery_sources.origin: Unbiased Science: Dissolving the Myths on Dishwasher and Laundry Pods (See What We Did There?)

discovery_sources.text: We do not have causative evidence that microplastics harm human health despite correlation claims in media
discovery_sources.origin: Unbiased Science: Dissolving the Myths on Dishwasher and Laundry Pods (See What We Did There?)
